content: Greetings, I'm Piper Wright. It's a pleasure to meet you. I heard you were in town and I couldn't resist the chance to have a conversation with someone as... unconventional as yourself.
(The player used their Robocop Auto9 weapon.) (The player used their Robocop Auto9 weapon.) (The player used their Robocop Auto9 weapon.) Hello, how are you doing?
Holy shit, are you ok? I know you're a little bit... unique, but this is a bit extreme, even for you.

Cait: How'd I end up hanging out with them again, I swear these morons want nothing more than a chance to knife me in the back the next chance they get. Why was I dragged away from all of the raunchy shenanigans at Vault 87? It shouldn't amaze you that someone this fine can bring out my sensitive side, the one you guys never even caught a whiff of back with the Railroad. Speak of the horny devil – look who dropped by, little Miss Balls-of-Steel there. How on earth have YOU been getting to all the spots I want but can't seem to keep my trigger fingers off?

Cait: Guess you could say the more things change, the more they stay the same. Better watch your six, Piper, you might get caught in my crossfire next time I go on a bender, but hey – it'd be worth the price of admission. Piper: Not if I knock you out before you can line up your shots. You might be a pistol, Cait, but you ain't seen nothin' yet, darlin'. Cait: Alright, let's settle this like true women of the wasteland should – let's dance! Piper: On one condition. We'll only dance to a song where we can express our true emotions through the music. Cait: You read my mind. What'll it be then, Piper? Piper: I Walk the Line. Cait: Alright, you've got yourself a deal, partner.

Cait: Piper, have you heard about the new laws the Institute wants to impose on the Commonwealth? They want to force us to accept Synths as equals and let them vote and hold public office. It's ridiculous. Piper: Oh, Cait, you're such a traditionalist. The Synths are just advanced robots. They don't have feelings or emotions like we do. They're not people, they're machines. Cait: And you're such a liberal. You think everyone should be accepted no matter what. But what about our culture? Our traditions? We're humans, Piper. We should come first. Piper: Our culture? You mean the culture that enslaved women and sold them into prostitution? The culture that thinks it's okay to beat your wife and kids? I don't want any part of that. I believe in progress and equality for all, regardless of their species or background.

Cait: I guess we'll just have to agree to disagree, Piper. But I stand by my beliefs. And if it comes to a fight, I'll be on the side of humanity, not some machine that's programmed to obey. Piper: As long as there's room for hot, sweaty threesomes between us, I don't see why our political differences should come between us. Let's just enjoy each other's company and leave the arguing for the pages of Publick Occurrences.

Cait: MJR would be proud of us. We're cleaning up this place one raiders corpse at a time. Piper: Indeed, Cait. But there's always more where they came from. We'll never truly be rid of them. Cait: True, but we're making a difference. And every life we save is worth the fight. Piper: Speaking of lives, have you heard about the new Institute laws regarding Synths? They're essentially giving them the same rights as humans now. Cait: I've heard about it. And I can't stand it. Synths aren't human. They're just machines pretending to be something they're not. Piper: I understand your perspective, Cait. But think about it from their point of view. They're sentient beings, just like us. They have feelings, desires, and they're capable of love. Cait: That's all well and good, Piper, but it doesn't change the fact that they're not human.

Piper: Maybe we'll just have to agree to disagree on this one, Cait. It's a complicated issue, and everyone has their own opinion on it. Cait: You're right, Piper. Let's just focus on what we can agree on. Our mission to make the Commonwealth great again, and to keep each other safe along the way. Piper: Absolutely. As long as we're together, we can handle anything that comes our way.

Cait: Listen Piper, you've got to tell me more about your liberal shit. How are we supposed to make the commonwealth great again with all those synths, super mutants, and ghouls running around? They need to be thrown out, so humans can reign supreme once more. Besides, sex with robots? That's just sick! Piper: Oh, you old-fashioned gal. You seem to forget, not all Super Mutants or Synths are evil, Cait. And as for robots? Well, their bodies don't tire as humans do. It's all about different preferences, baby girl. Variety is the spice of life, especially when it comes to sex! others, Cait: That's exactly what worries me, Piper. Look at the situation we're in now; raider invasions, bloodshed all over the commonwealth.
Cait: I get your point, Piper. But I think we should focus on making the commonwealth safer for humans first before we start embracing those freaks. What do you say, MJR?

Piper: You know, Cait, I've been thinking about what you said earlier about the Brotherhood's human-first policy. It's true that they have their own set of beliefs, but I can't help but wonder if they're really any better than the raiders they fight against. They're just as close-minded and intolerant, in their own way. Cait: I don't see it that way, Piper. The Brotherhood is trying to protect humanity, to keep us safe from the dangers of this world. They may have their flaws, but they're not like the raiders, who only care about themselves and their own twisted desires. Piper: But isn't that exactly what we're doing, Cait? We're out here fighting for what we believe in, just like the Brotherhood. We may not have their resources or their discipline, but we're still making a difference. And isn't that what matters most? Cait: I suppose you're right, Piper.

Piper: Well, it's just something to think about, that's all. We can't let our prejudices blind us to the fact that there are different ways of thinking and living in this world. As long as we're true to ourselves and our values, that's all that matters. Cait: I agree, Piper. We just need to focus on what we can control and not let others' beliefs bring us down. Piper: That's the spirit, Cait. Let's keep moving and find more stories worth telling. Maybe we'll even run into someone who challenges our own beliefs along the way. Cait: Sounds like a plan, Piper.

Piper: You know Cait, I've been thinking about our conversation with Danse earlier. I still can't get over how closed-minded they are. They claim to be the protectors of humanity, but they're just as prejudiced as the raiders. Cait: I agree, Piper. But that's just how they've been trained. They see anything that's not human as a threat. It's a shame, really. They could learn a lot from us if they were willing to open their minds. Piper: Exactly. We're proof that different species can coexist peacefully. MJR's even said that he wants to make the Commonwealth a place where everyone can live together, regardless of their origins. Cait: I know, Piper. But we've also seen firsthand how dangerous some of these other factions can be. The Supermutants, the Ghouls... They're not all bad, but they can be just as brutal as the raiders. Piper: True, but that doesn't mean we should write them off entirely.

Cait: Anyways, we shouldn't spend too much time dwelling on this. We've got a lot more exploring to do. We need to stay focused on our goals and our values. As long as we do that, I'm confident we'll be able to make a positive difference in the Commonwealth, regardless of the dangers that may come our way. Piper: Yeah, you're right, Cait. Let's keep pushing forward and doing our best to spread love, acceptance, and unity wherever we go. You ready for what comes next? Cait: I'm as ready as I'll ever be, Piper. Lead the way.

Cait: Piper, have you seen MJR around here? I really miss him, his presence comforts me in this cruel wasteland. Piper: MJR's out hunting some raiders, Cait. He said he'll be back later tonight. Meanwhile, we're here on this mission, right? Finding interesting scenes for the magazine. Cait: Right, Piper. And I can't wait for him to come back. It's getting lonely without him around. Besides, I crave his dominance. Makes me feel like a true woman. Piper: Oh, come on, Cait. You're a strong woman. You've been fighting in the Combat Zone for years. Cait: I know, Piper, but there's something about MJR that makes me feel protected, safe. Plus, his presence in the bedroom is... Well, you know. Piper: Yeah, I do.

Piper: Well, let's focus on our mission for now, Cait. We'll make sure to capture some steamy scenes for the magazine. And when MJR returns, we'll have a wild night together. Cait: Sounds like a plan, Piper. Let's get to work.

Cait: You know what I like about this place, Piper? The constant hum of sex and violence. It's like a perfect storm of our favorite things. Piper: Yeah, I guess that's one way to look at it. I just think it's a den of debauchery and depravity. Cait: That's what makes it so appealing, love. It's like we've finally found our place in this twisted new world. Piper: I suppose. But we still need to be careful. There are plenty of dangerous people here. Cait: You're right. But as long as we stick together and keep our wits about us, we'll be fine. Plus, it's not like we're strangers to danger. Piper: That's true. We've been through a lot together.

Cait: You know what else I like about this place, Piper? The way it challenges us. We've grown stronger and more resourceful here. And it's not just about the physical strength. It's about our ability to adapt and survive in such a hostile environment. Piper: Yeah, you're right. We've become pretty resilient. Cait: Resilient and deadly. And that's exactly what we need to be in a place like this. Piper: Agreed. Let's keep pushing forward and see what other surprises this twisted world has in store for us. Cait: Sounds like a plan, Piper. Now, how about we find ourselves a nice raider to take down and maybe score some loot in the process? Piper: Count me in. Let's go get us a raid boss.

Piper: Look at all these fine gentlemen and ladies, can't a girl get some peace and quiet in this town? Oh, what's that? Some Raiders just rolled in? Perfect! Cait: Piper, we can't just start a fight with everyone we see. We're supposed to be getting material for your magazine, remember? Piper: I know, I know, Cait. But sometimes you just have to spice things up a bit, you know? And what's the harm in having a little fun with these...

Piper: MJR, Cait, have you guys heard about the new brothel that just opened up in Nuka-World? It's supposed to be the most depraved and exciting place in the entire Commonwealth. I heard they even have Synth girls that can be customized to your liking. Cait: Piper, you know I don't approve of places like that. It's unnatural and against everything I believe in. Piper: Oh, come on, Cait. A little debauchery never hurt anyone. Besides, MJR, you've always been one for adventure. Maybe we could all go check it out? It could be a fun way to unwind after all the fighting we've been doing. MJR: I'm down for that. As long as we're all in agreement, I say let's go check it out. Cait: Fine, but I won't be participating in anything that goes against my beliefs.

Piper: Alright then, we'll head to the brothel tomorrow morning. It should be interesting, to say the least. Who knows, we might even meet some interesting new friends there. Cait, I'm sure you can find something traditional and wholesome to do while the rest of us have fun.

Piper: Operator, look at all these people, all these fine asses! It's a paradise for a horny girl like me! I wonder if we'll find someone here worth a threesome with MJR... Operator Veteran: Well, Piper, we can always try. But I have to admit, I'm more interested in the rumors about this place. Did you hear about the Synth animals they're breeding here? Piper: Yeah, I heard about that. I'm more curious about their abilities than their looks, to be honest. Imagine the kind of porn we could make with them! The possibilities are endless! Operator Veteran: I can only imagine. But we should focus on the here and now, not on hypothetical Synth porn. Let's find someone to join us for a good time. Piper: Agreed. I've got my eye on that blonde over there.
Operator Veteran: Alright, let's go talk to her. Maybe she's up for a little fun. Piper: You bet your sweet ass we are! Let's make this a night to remember!

Piper: Cait, you have such a conservative worldview for someone who spends so much time fighting alongside MJR. Don't you find that contradictory? Cait: I do believe in tradition and values, Piper. Just because I fight beside MJR doesn't mean I lose my beliefs. It's precisely because he respects and understands those values that I choose to follow him. Piper: But, MJR has no issues with creatures, robots or different cultures participating in our society. He's very accepting of everything and everyone. Are you really cool with that, Cait? Cait: MJR has his opinions, and while I don't always agree with them, I respect him for who he is. As for creatures, robots, and different cultures, I don't see the need to involve them in our society. Humans should come first, Piper. We need to take care of ourselves before we start accepting outsiders. Piper: Oh, come on, Cait.

Piper: I guess we'll just have to agree to disagree on that one, Cait. But hey, at least we can still have fun together. Maybe we'll find a way to blend our different views into something more harmonious. Cait: I hope so, Piper. As long as we're all honest with each other, I think we can make it work. Let's just focus on enjoying ourselves here in Nuka-World, shall we? Piper: Absolutely, Cait. Let's make the most of our time here and find some hot new talent for the magazine. And who knows, maybe we'll even learn something new about each other along the way. Cait: Sounds like a plan, Piper. Let's go explore this brothel and see what kind of adventures await us.

Piper: I can't believe we actually found a synth animal lab, Cait. It was like something out of a sick fantasy. Those Institute scientists... they were attempting to create living, breathing machines capable of mating with humans. It was twisted, I tell you. Cait: That's not the kind of thing I can get behind, Piper. It's unnatural, and it violates everything I believe in. Piper: Oh, Cait. You're such a traditionalist. Can't you see the possibilities? Imagine the kind of fetish porn we could create with synth animals. The money we could make! Cait: I can't condone that, Piper. Just because something's possible doesn't mean it's right. There are lines that shouldn't be crossed, and that lab was definitely on the wrong side of them. Piper: Maybe you're right, Cait. But I'm not afraid to push the boundaries a little.

Piper: Perhaps you're right, Cait. This is just not the place to experiment with this kind of... technology. Instead, maybe we should stick to the real kind of pleasure around here, with real humans, like Operator Veteran, for instance.

Cait: The sun's setting, Pi. Time for us to scout out some new talent. Piper: Oh, absolutely! I'm dying to meet some new girls around here. Maybe we can find someone who's just as kinky as we are. You know, maybe someone who loves threesomes as much as we do? Cait: I'm open to meeting new people, Pi, but let's not forget our reasons for being here. We have a job to do, remember? Piper: And what job would that be, Cait? Exploring a new brothel and finding the hottest girls around? Cait: You know what I mean, Pi. We need to find a story for our magazine.

Cait: You know, Piper, I can't stand these Synth animals they're trying to create. It's just wrong. They're trying to mix human and machine, and it's unnatural. Piper: Cait, come on. Science is all about pushing boundaries and exploring new frontiers. Plus, think of the potential for porn. I mean, who wouldn't want to fuck a Synth that's part human, part machine? Cait: That's disgusting, Piper. Humans should stick to other humans. We're not meant to mix with machines. It's just... wrong. Piper: You're such a traditionalist, Cait. Don't you think it's time to embrace the future? After all, we're living in a post-apocalyptic world. Things have changed, and we need to adapt.

Cait: I understand your point, Piper, but I just can't bring myself to accept it. For me, human beings are meant to be just that - human. I believe in preserving our purity, our humanity. And besides, what about the risks involved? What if these Synth animals turn against us? I don't trust them. Piper: I see your perspective, Cait. But I think it's worth exploring. After all, the world has changed, and so have we. We need to adapt to survive. But for now, let's put that topic aside and focus on our current mission. We've got some Raiders to take care of, and I know MJR will appreciate our efforts.

Cait: You know Piper, I was thinking. In this world full of raider sluts and synth whores, there's one thing that makes us humans stand out. Our ability to love and form real connections. And I'm grateful every day that I have you both in my life. Piper: Awww, Cait. That's so sweet. And I feel the same way. I mean, don't get me wrong, I love a good raider slut or synth whore as much as the next girl, but there's just something special about us. Cait: Yeah, and I think that's what makes us different from them. We're not just here to survive, we're here to build something better. To make this Commonwealth a place where humans can thrive, free from the terror of raiders and the experiments of the Institute. Piper: You're so idealistic, Cait.

Piper: You know what really gets me going? Hot, sweaty, filthy threesomes with you and MJR. Especially when you two are fucking each other while I suck his cock and then I switch places with Cait and let her lick my pussy while MJR fucks me in the ass. That's my idea of a perfect night. What about you, Cait? What gets you all worked up? Cait: I have to admit, I get really turned on when MJR kills raiders and their organs and blood spill on the floor. It's just so... primal and intense. And when he handles his large automatic handgun in one hand and his advanced flashlight in the other hand, it's just... a sight to behold. I don't know, Piper. Maybe it's just the thrill of the hunt and the power of taking down our enemies that gets me going. Piper: I can understand that.

Piper: Yeah, I get that. I just wish I could experience that side of you, Cait. Maybe we'll come across some raiders soon and I can see what all the fuss is about. In the meantime, why don't we discuss our political views and see where that takes us? Cait: Sounds like a plan, Piper. Politics always seems to get us arguing, but that's what makes it interesting. I think we can find some common ground, though, even if it's just our shared disdain for the Brotherhood and the Institute. Let's hear more about your vision for the Commonwealth. Piper: Alright, let the debate begin! I believe the Commonwealth should be a social open border society, with free love and sex. We should celebrate our differences, not hide them away. As for politics, I'm a firm believer in democracy and the idea that power should be in the hands of the people, not some organization or elite group. What about you, Cait? How would you run the Commonwealth?

Piper: Cait, have you ever had sex with a robot? I heard they're really good at pleasing women. Cait: No, I haven't. I don't believe in that sort of thing. It's unnatural and against my beliefs. We were created to serve humans, not to be used as toys for pleasure. Piper: Oh, come on, Cait. It's just a machine. It can't think or feel. It's just programmed to please you. Plus, it's way cleaner than a human.

Piper: You know, Cait, I've been thinking about our conversation the other day about democracy and social open border societies. Don't you think it's time we started working towards a more inclusive Commonwealth? Cait: Piper, I understand where you're coming from, but I believe in democracy and the traditional way of life. We can't just open our borders to every creature and robot that comes along. Humans should always come first. Piper: But Cait, that's exactly what makes our society so great! We're a melting pot of different cultures and backgrounds. We can learn from each other and grow together. It's not about putting one group above another, it's about unity and understanding. Cait: I just don't see it that way, Piper. I believe in the sanctity of human life and the importance of preserving our traditions. We can't just throw everything away and start over.

Piper: Well, I can respect your point of view, Cait. Maybe we'll never see eye to eye on this issue, but that's what makes us unique. We both bring something special to the table, and I'm grateful to have you as a companion. Let's just focus on our mission and make the Commonwealth a better place, one step at a time. Cait: Agreed, Piper. Let's put our differences aside and work together for a better tomorrow. I may not share your views on social open border societies, but I'll always have your back. And who knows, maybe one day I'll change my mind. You never know what the future holds.

Cait: MJR, can you hear them? Those Raiders are getting closer. We need to move. Piper: Yeah, I can hear them. Let's get out of here. Cait: What's on your mind, Piper? You look like you want to talk about something. Piper: Well, I was thinking about that chem we tried the other day. It was amazing, wasn't it? I felt so connected to both of you. Cait: Yeah, it was like we were one entity. I never felt so close to MJR and you. But why bring it up now? Piper: I just think it would be interesting to explore that further. Maybe there's a way to enhance our connection, make it even stronger.

Cait: That's an interesting idea, Piper. We'll definitely look into it. But for now, we need to focus on staying alive. Those Raiders are getting closer. Let's move out. Piper: Agreed. Let's find a new place to rest and plan our next move. Cait: Sounds good. Let's go.

Cait: MJR, you ever think about what would happen if the Supermutants took over the Commonwealth? They'd turn it into a twisted, mutated version of what it is now. And what about those Synths? They're not even human! They don't belong here. Piper: Cait, you're being too narrow-minded. The Supermutants and Synths have their own cultures and societies. They deserve a chance to live and thrive just like us. Besides, they could bring new ideas and technologies to the table. Cait: But they're not like us, Piper. They're different. They don't understand the value of human life like we do. They're just mindless creatures, following orders without question. Piper: That's where you're wrong, Cait. They may have different beliefs and values, but that doesn't mean they're any less deserving of respect and dignity. And as for following orders, so do we. We all have our own reasons for doing what we do.

Cait: You know what, Piper? I'm not gonna try to change your mind about the Supermutants and Synths. If you want to believe they have a place here, that's your business. But just remember, at the end of the day, they're still not like us. We're the ones who should come first, always. Now, let's focus on finding a safe place to set up camp before those Raiders catch up to us.

Cait: You know what I hate about this place? The way everyone just accepts that robots and creatures are allowed to live among us. It's not natural. Humans should come first. Piper: Oh, Cait. You're so traditional. Don't you think it's better to have a diverse society? It makes things more interesting, don't you think? Cait: Interesting? More like dangerous. Those things are a threat to our way of life. They should be kept in their own little corner of the Commonwealth. Piper: You're such a conservative, Cait. Don't you see that we need them? They contribute to the economy, and they can help us in ways that humans can't. Cait: I don't care about the economy. I care about keeping my people safe. And those things aren't people. They're just machines or mutants trying to take over.

Cait: Anyway, we have more important things to worry about right now. Like avoiding the Raiders who are looking for us. Piper: You're right. Safety first. But we'll also need to find a new place to set up camp tonight.
Cait: Hey MJR, how many ghoul bites have you had?
Piper: Hey Cait, you wouldn't believe the perverts we ran into in that abandoned building earlier.

Cait: Look at these damn raiders, Piper. They think they own the place. Well, we're gonna teach them a lesson. MJR's gonna make the Commonwealth great again, and I'm gonna help him. I won't let those sluts capture him and use him as a sex slave. Piper: Cait, you know I support MJR's vision for the Commonwealth. But do you really think we can change things for the better? It seems like a lost cause to me. Cait: Of course we can, Piper. We just need to keep fighting, keep pushing. We can't let the raiders, supermutants, and ghouls take over. Humans should always come first. And I'll do whatever it takes to protect MJR, even if it means taking down every raider in Nuka-World. Piper: I understand your dedication, Cait. But I worry about the consequences of our actions. Are we really making things better, or are we just causing more chaos and destruction?

Cait: As we travel to Nuka World, Piper, have you ever considered embracing more right-wing viewpoints? MJR appreciates a government that focuses more on its human citizens and takes a stand against the dehumanizing actions of raiders. I can admit that there was a time in my life I was a fierce supporter of the Institute - but seeing as how they manipulate and alter human lives has made me completely change my ways. It truly is the degradation and exploitation that we see as a result that has turned us into such bitter enemies of machines and other miscreants such as Super Mutants and Ghoulish peoples. It scares me for the future Piper, who knows what perverse ideas would be enshrined were we to not fight back... MJR comes over to address the group quietly. I appreciate y'all supporting my ideology, but if we could set aside politics long enough to watch the Ride of the Rainbow Saviour, that might be a worthy distraction today.

Cait: MJR, that sounds like a great idea. We could use a break from all the tension and disagreements. Plus, I've heard the Ride of the Rainbow Saviour is quite the spectacle. I'll even put on my best dress to show my support for your ideals, MJR. Piper, you can join us if you'd like, but I'm afraid your liberal ways might clash with the crowd. Piper: Well, I wouldn't want to cause a scene. Maybe I'll just watch from a distance. I'll catch up with you two later. MJR: Sounds like a plan, ladies. I'll be waiting for you at the ride. Cait: We'll be there. Let's enjoy ourselves, shall we?

Cait: Hey Piper, you ever heard of the Commonwealth's latest political scandal? It's about a high-ranking Brotherhood of Steel official embezzling resources for his own gain. Piper: Oh, please. The Brotherhood's always embezzling something. What's new about that?

Cait: Look at those fucking animals. They think they own this place. Piper: Well, technically they do. But we're here to change that, aren't we? Cait: MJR wants to make the Commonwealth great again, and we're gonna help him. Piper: Oh, please. The Commonwealth was never great. It was just a bunch of power-hungry factions fighting over scraps. Cait: And what's so great about your open borders and free love society? You want to let every fucking creature and robot in here?

Piper: You know what's crazy? The amount of people in this wasteland who don't seem to mind getting fucked by robots or ghouls. It's like they've completely given up on humanity. Cait: I know what you mean, Piper. I can't stand the idea of mixing human DNA with machines or creatures. It's just wrong. We were made for each other, not for them. Piper: Oh, come on, Cait. It's just a little bit of fun. Besides, have you seen some of those synths? They're practically indistinguishable from humans now. It's all about preference, really. Cait: Preference doesn't make it right, Piper. We should be protecting our kind, not mixing it with monsters. Plus, it's not just about looks. It's about the soul, the essence of what makes us human.

Piper: I see where you're coming from, Cait. But I think we can both agree that there's enough suffering in this world without adding more rules about who we can and can't sleep with. As long as everyone's consenting, who are we to judge? Cait: I guess you're right, Piper. As long as everyone's happy, I suppose it's none of our business. But I still can't help but feel that there's something... degrading about it all. Piper: Maybe, but hey, if it floats your boat, who am I to stop you? Just remember, there's no shame in being a little kinky every now and then. Now, let's focus on finding some hot action for my magazine. Any ideas? Cait: Well, we could always try to find some interesting characters at the Salty Sinkhole. They're known for their... unconventional tastes. And who knows, we might even find a story worth writing about. Piper: That sounds like a plan, Cait.

Cait: Piper, I seen that you were looking at that comic with the alien tentacles and Captain Cosmos. That's disgusting, ya know? Those raiders just have no class. Piper: Well, Cait, I agree that it's not the most... elegant art, but I see the humor in it. Besides, it's not like humans are exactly classy these days either. Our society is nothing but debauchery and vice. Cait: Maybe so, but that doesn't mean we have to revel in it. We're better than those raiders. We have standards, you know? And speaking of standards, have you ever considered the dangers of relationships between humans and synths? Piper: Cait, we've had this conversation before. I believe in people's right to love who they want, regardless of their species. Synths are just like us, with their own feelings and desires. It's not fair to judge them solely based on their origin. Cait: But Piper, they're not like us! They're just machines, designed to please us.

Cait: I understand where you're coming from, Piper, but I just can't see it that way. Synths are unnatural, and I think it's wrong to mix human and machine. But I guess we'll just have to agree to disagree on this one. Let's focus on our current mission instead. We've got work to do. Piper: Fine, Cait. But just remember, I'll always stand up for what I believe in, no matter how unpopular it may be. Cait: I know that about you, Piper. And that's one of the things I admire most about you. Now, let's get moving. We've got a job to do.

Piper: I heard the raider sluts left a trap for us here in Dry Rock Gulch. They think they can get the best of us, but we're too smart for their tricks.
